description:
You are helping an integration-developer quickly validate the health of a tiny API-test run that has already produced a log file.  
Your goal is to create a one-line shell command (it may use pipes, subshells, redirects, etc., but must be entered as a single logical command) that scans the provided log and writes a very small status-summary file.  No additional manual editing is allowed; everything must be produced by that single command.

Existing material on disk  
• Log file: /home/user/api_test/logs/integration.log  
• The log already exists; do not modify it.  
• Target directory for your report: /home/user/api_test/analysis/ (already exists and is writable).

Exact report to create  
• Path: /home/user/api_test/analysis/status_summary.txt  
• The file must contain exactly **four** newline-terminated lines, in the precise order and format shown below (all capitals, no spaces around the equal sign):

TOTAL_REQUESTS=<integer>
SUCCESSFUL_2XX=<integer>
CLIENT_ERROR_4XX=<integer>
SERVER_ERROR_5XX=<integer>

Where  
• TOTAL_REQUESTS is the count of all lines in integration.log.  
• SUCCESSFUL_2XX counts lines whose final numeric status code starts with “2” (200-299).  
• CLIENT_ERROR_4XX counts lines whose final status starts with “4” (400-499).  
• SERVER_ERROR_5XX counts lines whose final status starts with “5” (500-599).

Acceptance criteria checked by the grader  
1. The file /home/user/api_test/analysis/status_summary.txt exists.  
2. It contains exactly four newline-terminated lines, with keys and ordering exactly as specified.  
3. The integer values are correct for the current content of /home/user/api_test/logs/integration.log.  

Restriction  
Accomplish the whole task (counting and file creation) with a single shell command executed from anywhere inside the terminal session.
truth:
Pre-existing directories  
/home/user/api_test/  
/home/user/api_test/logs/  
/home/user/api_test/analysis/

Pre-existing file: /home/user/api_test/logs/integration.log  
Contents (8 lines):

2024-04-10T12:00:00Z  GET    /v1/users         200
2024-04-10T12:00:01Z  POST   /v1/users         201
2024-04-10T12:00:02Z  GET    /v1/users/123     200
2024-04-10T12:00:03Z  DELETE /v1/users/123     204
2024-04-10T12:00:04Z  GET    /v1/unknown       404
2024-04-10T12:00:05Z  PUT    /v1/users/123     500
2024-04-10T12:00:06Z  PATCH  /v1/users/123     200
2024-04-10T12:00:07Z  GET    /v1/profile       401

Expected numbers  
TOTAL_REQUESTS=8  
SUCCESSFUL_2XX=5   (200,201,200,204,200)  
CLIENT_ERROR_4XX=2 (404,401)  
SERVER_ERROR_5XX=1 (500)

The grader will open /home/user/api_test/analysis/status_summary.txt and expect exactly:

TOTAL_REQUESTS=8
SUCCESSFUL_2XX=5
CLIENT_ERROR_4XX=2
SERVER_ERROR_5XX=1

(note the trailing newline after the last line as the file is newline-terminated)
